Ntc Of Mtn Against Deft Wright To Order And Admissions Deemed Admitted Or Alt. Compel Further Responses And Further Responses To The First Set Of Form Interrogatories And Award Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-14-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance:
William S. Weisberg, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Brian J. Hecktman
Elizabeth R. Wright, defendant and opposing party, in Propria Persona
After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted as modified.
Plaintiff Hecktman's Motion Against Defendant Wright To Order (1) Admissions Deemed Admitted Or Alternatively Compel Further Responses; And (2) Further Responses To The First Set Of Form Interrogatories; And (3) Award Sanctions granted in part, denied in part:
1. Motion for order that requests for admission be deemed admitted denied
2. Motion in the alternative to compel further response to request for admissions, denied in part, granted in part:
a. Denied as to request for admission nos. 2-15, 18-22;
b. As to request for admission nos. 1, 16, 17 granted; defendant shall serve a verified, code compliant, supplemental response within ten days of notice of entry of this order
3. Motion in the alternative to compel further response first set of form interrogatories, granted, as follows:
a. Within seven days of hearing of this motion, the parties shall meet and confer to agree on definitions of the terms "incident" and "agreement" for purposes of discovery in this action. Defendant thereafter shall provide full, complete, and verified supplemental responses to plaintiffs' first set of form interrogatories within ten days;
b. In preparing her supplemental responses, defendant shall ensure that her responses are code compliant, with particular reference to form interrogatory no. 17.1, which requires a separate response for each referenced response to request for admissions.
4. Request for sanctions denied as to plaintiff's request for admissions; granted as to plaintiff's form interrogatories. Sanctions shall issue against defendant Wright in the sum of $290.00, payable within thirty days of notice of entry of this order. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.
